panels.title: Total OTLP requests / min
panels.description: Across all connectors and signals. Sum of /v1/logs + /v1/metrics + /v1/traces requests in the last 5 minutes, scaled to per-minute. A flat-line at zero on a host where codex/claudecode is supposed to be running observability mode means the gateway is not receiving telemetry.

panels.title: OTel records / min
panels.description: Leaf records (logRecords + dataPoints + spans) extracted from inbound batches. Drives the per-connector volume time series below.

panels.title: Codex turns / min
panels.description: agent-turn-complete + other notify events posted by codex's notify-bridge.

panels.title: Malformed-payload rate
panels.description: Fraction of inbound OTLP-HTTP bodies the receiver rejected for failed JSON parsing. >10% triggers DefenseClawConnectorTelemetryMalformed.

panels.title: Loudest source
panels.description: Connector source emitting the most OTel log records. Helps spot a runaway codex or claudecode session.

panels.title: Quietest source (silence sec)
panels.description: Maximum seconds since the gateway received an OTel batch from any connector. >600s triggers DefenseClawConnectorTelemetrySilent.

panels.title: Requests per second by source / signal
panels.description: Rate of OTLP-HTTP batches accepted, broken out by connector source and signal type. Drift between codex and claudecode rates is expected (claudecode emits logs+metrics; codex emits logs+metrics+traces).

panels.title: Records per second (volume) by source / signal
panels.description: Leaf records (logRecords / data points / spans) per second. Use this rather than request rate to compare connector verbosity since one batch can carry hundreds of records.

panels.title: Bytes per second by source / signal
panels.description: Inbound body size. Spikes correlate with verbose tool-use prompts or large completions.

panels.title: Per-source ingest mix
panels.description: Last-5m rate per (source, signal, result). Lets you eyeball which connectors are emitting which signals and whether any are throwing malformed.

panels.title: Notify events per second by type / result
panels.description: Codex-only notify-bridge events. This stays at zero when Claude Code is the active connector. Result=malformed means the bridge sent a body that did not parse as JSON.

targets.expr: sum by (type, result) (rate(defenseclaw_codex_notify_total[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)

panels.title: Notify events by type + status
panels.description: Last-5m totals by codex's `type` and `status` fields. status=success vs status=failed lets you spot turns the model gave up on.

targets.expr: sort_desc(sum by (type, status, result) (increase(defenseclaw_codex_notify_total[5m])) or on() vector(0))

panels.title: Gateway hook invocations / sec
panels.description: Hook requests that reached the gateway, grouped by connector/event/result. Client-side spawn failures happen before this metric; they show in the agent UI and should disappear after stale hooks are removed or the client restarts.

panels.title: OTel log records / sec by source
panels.description: Inbound /v1/logs records. Compare with the hook panel to spot drift.

panels.title: Tokens / sec by connector + direction
panels.description: OTel GenAI semconv counter (gen_ai.client.token.usage). Codex token counts are promoted from its response.completed OTLP log events; Claude Code token counts are promoted from claude_code.token.usage; proxy traffic records token usage directly.

targets.expr: sum by (gen_ai_agent_name, gen_ai_token_type) (rate(gen_ai_client_token_usage_sum[$__rate_interval])) or on() vector(0)

panels.title: LLM operation duration p95 by service
panels.description: OTel GenAI gen_ai.client.operation.duration. This stays at zero until native GenAI duration histograms arrive.

targets.expr: histogram_quantile(0.95, sum by (le, service_name) (rate(gen_ai_client_operation_duration_seconds_bucket[$__rate_interval]))) or on() vector(0)

panels.title: Connector telemetry log stream
panels.description: OTel log records emitted by the gateway when it accepts an OTLP batch or connector hook/notify event. Search by action=otel.ingest.*, codex.notify.*, codex-hook, or claude-code-hook.
